summary:
The infant tends to be smaller at birth because of increased intrauterine exposure to elevated blood sugar from the mother; additionally, beta-agonist medications should not be used by diabetic women during labor due to potential adverse effects related to diabetes management. ```

The summary captures both critical aspects regarding the impact on newborn size and medication safety concerns pertinent to managing deliveries involving diabetic patients accurately within two concise statements. ```
```markdown
Key Points Summarized:

Infants born to diabetic mothers tend to be smaller due to excessive prenatal insulin production, while β-agonist drugs must be avoided during such pregnancies to prevent exacerbating maternal glycemic control issues.
```
